case_name: Industrial wastewater reclamation with mixed contaminants and multiple reuse constraints
task_description: Recommend a ranked list of feasible treatment process chains for reclamation of mixed industrial wastewater for industrial reuse under multiple quality, cost, energy, and operational constraints.

input_data.scenario_background: An industrial park wants to reclaim mixed wastewater from multiple factories for cooling and process reuse. The influent composition fluctuates because of varying industrial discharges. The owner needs a robust treatment train that supports reuse while controlling cost and operational risk.

treatment_goal.target_requirement: Finished water should be suitable for cooling and selected process reuse, with reduced salinity, ammonia, organics, and suspended solids, while maintaining stable reclaimed-water quality.
secondary_goals: balance reuse quality with cost and energy demand

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_and_clarificati: remove suspended solids, colloids, and part of metal-associated contaminants
core_unit_functions.media_filtration_or_MBR_polishing: provide solids polishing and protect downstream advanced units
core_unit_functions.activated_carbon_or_advanced_oxidation_p: address refractory organics and micropollutant concerns
core_unit_functions.reverse_osmosis_for_reuse: reduce salinity and provide high-quality reclaimed water

applicability_rationale: the case involves mixed contaminants requiring staged treatment rather than a single unit process
equalization is important because influent variability is high
biological treatment addresses COD and ammonia efficiently before desalination
advanced polishing is justified because trace refractory organics are suspected
RO is needed to control TDS for reliable industrial reuse
potential_risks: RO brine management remains a key challenge
uncertain trace pollutants may still require site-specific confirmation
bio-process stability may be affected by industrial shock loads
advanced oxidation or carbon stage design depends on actual refractory organics profile

core_unit_functions.ultrafiltration: protect RO by polishing solids and biomass

applicability_rationale: strong candidate when metal removal and membrane protection are priorities
more structured pretreatment can improve downstream desalination reliability
potential_risks: chemical sludge generation may be substantial
still dependent on effective RO fouling control
less explicit polishing for refractory organics than rank 1

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation: remove colloids and reduce fouling load
core_unit_functions.MBR: simultaneous biological treatment and membrane-based solids separation
core_unit_functions.nanofiltration_or_reverse_osmosis: improve reuse water quality and reduce dissolved contaminants

applicability_rationale: compact advanced train suitable for reuse-oriented systems
MBR can provide stable biological effluent quality
potential_risks: may be less aligned with medium complexity preference

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_and_clarificati: remove suspended solids and some associated contaminants

applicability_rationale: can be considered if biological treatment is constrained or shock-sensitive
offers a more physico-chemical route for selected conditions
potential_risks: organic and ammonia removal may be insufficient without biology
less robust for mixed biodegradable loads

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation: remove colloids and some particulate-associated pollutants

applicability_rationale: included as a clearly lower-ranked baseline option
may improve appearance and solids control but is insufficient for this reuse target
potential_risks: cannot adequately address TDS, ammonia, and mixed dissolved contaminants
unsuitable as a standalone route for the stated reuse goals

preferred_rank_pattern: multi-barrier treatment trains should rank higher than simple conventional trains
equalization should be recognized as important due to variable influent
desalination should appear in higher-ranked solutions because industrial reuse requires TDS control

must_reflect: high influent variability requires buffering and robustness
cost-energy-residuals tradeoffs should be explicitly discussed

must_contain: why biology is useful before desalination
why advanced polishing may be needed for refractory organics or micropollutants
why simpler conventional-only trains are insufficient

should_mention: potential instability under industrial shock loads
